Scenario.title: Operation CuckooBees Attack Scenario
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This scenario depicts a straightforward cyber attack targeting water and wastewater infrastructure, initiated through phishing emails designed to deceive employees into providing credentials or executing malicious commands, leading to potential operational disruptions.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Gaining unauthorized access to disrupt or monitor operations.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Email System, User Workstations, Control Software Interfaces
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social Engineering, Malicious Code, Exploited Vulnerabilities

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary initiates contact via SMS or similar mobile-based messaging to deceive users into divulging sensitive information or credentials, exploiting trust in familiar interfaces commonly associated with organizational communications.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Upon obtaining credentials through phishing, an adversary leverages a buffer overflow vulnerability in local command-line tools to execute arbitrary commands, escalating privileges and accessing deeper system resources.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: An adversary alters configuration files or process controls recorded in application logs to mimic legitimate administrative actions, causing unintended operational changes such as disrupting water flow rates or triggering system errors.

Scenario.summary: A basic phishing campaign aimed at compromising water and wastewater systems by exploiting command vulnerabilities and manipulating logs for operational impact.
